Formative Presentations · II

Game Localization in

A comparative analysis of stylistic transfer in the
internal monologues — human translation vs. machine.

Mustafa Demirel  ·  Başak İlhan  ·  Emre Canal
ETI 499 Spring 2026 Izmir University of Economics
→   use arrows to advance
§ 01   /   Source Text

Why Disco Elysium?

A game where the protagonist's own mind is the cast.

Disco Elysium
ZA/UM  ·  2019  ·  RPG

A dialogue-driven RPG with no combat. The player controls an amnesiac detective whose every action is arbitrated by text.

24 inner voices representing aspects of the protagonist's mind drive gameplay through text.

Among them, three voices are the most stylistically distinctive — ideal lenses for examining how style survives translation.

ARB
Ancient
Reptilian Brain
Primal dread, hostility, pre-verbal urges. Fragmented, guttural.
LS
Limbic
System
Raw affect, cravings, panic. Urgent, breathless, theatrical.
IE
Inland
Empire
The surreal, the animistic. Dream-logic, metaphor, things that shouldn't speak speaking.
§ 02   /   Research Focus

From field to project.

Narrowing a broad question into a testable one.

General Focus
Game Localization
Specific Lens
Stylistic Transfer & Register in RPG Dialogue
Project Topic
A Comparative Analysis of Stylistic Transfer in Disco Elysium's Internal Monologues Ancient Reptilian Brain  ·  Limbic System  ·  Inland Empire Official Turkish Localization  vs.  Machine Translation
§ 03   /   Theoretical Framework

Two lenses, one verdict.

Mechanics describe what the translator did. Deformation judges what it cost.

Theoretical Framework
Antoine Berman
Stylistic impact.

Twelve deforming tendencies that quietly flatten a foreign text: how each choice dulls, smooths or amplifies the original voice.

rationalization clarification ennoblement qualitative impoverishment destruction of rhythm + 7 more
applied to three inner voices

The final verdict: whether the game's distinctive voice reaches the Turkish-speaking audience intact.

§ 04   /   A Finding

One line, four versions.

A small moment where style survives — or doesn't.

Limbic System
“Yes, you're one disco mother.”

A playful, gendered address — faintly ridiculous, consciously strange. The kind of line a character says at a woman, not to her.

The official Turkish flattens the feminine into a generic çocuk. The machine, pedantically literal, gives us disko anası — grammatically faithful, stylistically deaf.

Our proposal restores the wink: altın günü kadını — a culturally-anchored register that keeps the playful-feminine teasing intact.

Per Berman · HT commits rationalization; AT commits qualitative impoverishment.
English TR Official TR Machine (Opus) Proposed Translation
LS Infinitesimally small. Ölçemeyeceğin kadar. Sonsuz derecede küçük. İncir çekirdeğini doldurmaz.
ARB Coming right up, sir. Smooth passage... Hemen geliyor, efendim... Derhal hazır, beyefendi... Baş üstüne abim. Tekerine...
ARB All right. Nothingtown to Fuck-All-Borough! Pekâlâ. Hiçlikbaşı... Pekala. Hiçlik-kasabasından... Peki madem. Boktan bir...
LS Yes, you're one disco mother. Evet. Tam bir disko çocuğusun. Evet, sen bir disko anasısın. Evet, tam bir altın günü kadınısın.
LS You can't even make out it's a ball, when you're dukin' it out. It's that large. Didişmekle meşgulken top olduğunu... Birbirinize girerken top olduğunu... Can havliyle debelenirken onun...
ARB Do you want me to upgrade that into a *one way trip*, sir? *Tek yön bilete* yükseltme yapmamı ister... Bunu bir *tek yönlü bilet*e yükseltmemi... Bunu sizin için "tek yön tahtalı köy"...
IE An ominous, foreboding feeling fills you, as you look at the tie. Kravata bakarken, içine uğursuz... Kravata bakarken içini uğursuz... Kravata bakarken içine darallar...
01  /  05
